content: Biological and chemical pathogenic agents (the original, non-AI definition of agents) can be framed as naturally occurring autonomous agents driven by a primitive form of ICM-style intrinsic curiosity, where they gain 'internal reward' from encountering and colonizing unexploited new host niches rather than just responding to external environmental triggers. This re-framing suggests we can adapt ICM design from 2025 reinforcement learning to model and predict pathogen spillover events into new human populations.
